content.text:
Verified against git. I have to correct the record before we build on a wrong assumption — you asked me to push back, so here are the facts from **this repo** (`main`, fully in sync with `origin/main`):

## ⚠️ Reality check: the code for 23–27 isn't here

| You said | What the repo actually shows |
|---|---|
| "26–27 is done and pushed" | `db/schema.ts`, `connection.ts`, all repos + both services = **1-line stubs**. No `better-sqlite3`/`drizzle` installed. Nothing in git history. |
| "aggregation is done" | Only **cycle 22** exists — and it's the hardcoded `return "APPROVED"` stub *I* wrote this session, **still uncommitted**. No 23/24/25 tests. |
| Latest commit (all branches) | `d958393 feat: add visit and dollar limit handling` — that's cycles 14–17. Nothing after. |

**Most likely explanation:** the **design** for all of this *is* locked and pushed — `docs/adjudication-plan.md` + `docs/domain-model.md` fully spec aggregation, the writeback, and disputes. That's probably the "done/pushed" you're remembering. The **implementation** isn't written yet.

👉 If you have this code on **another branch, clone, or machine**, point me at it and I'll pull/inspect instead of duplicating. Otherwise, we're building it fresh — and your plan ("validate tables → then create schema") is exactly right, with **nothing to clobber**.

## ✅ Table-validation pass (what you asked for)

Here are the **9 tables** from the locked model. Validate each before I write a line of DDL:

| # | Table | Purpose | Key columns |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `members` | insured person | `id`, `name`🔒, `dob`🔒 |
| 2 | `policies` | coverage for a plan year | `id`, `member_id`, `plan_year`, `effective_date`, `termination_date`, `deductible_cents`, `oop_max_cents` |
| 3 | `coverage_rules` | how one service is covered | `policy_id`, `service_code`, `covered`, `excluded`, `cost_share`, `applies_deductible`, `limit`, `requires_prior_auth` |
| 4 | `claims` | submission grouping | `id`, `member_id`, `service_date`, `provider`🔒, `diagnosis_code`🔒, `status` |
| 5 | `line_items` | unit of adjudication | `id`, `claim_id`, `service_code`, `billed_cents`, `units`, `prior_auth_present`, `status`, `fingerprint` |
| 6 | `adjudications` | immutable decision (append-only) | `id`, `line_item_id`, `status`, `payable_cents`, `member_responsibility_cents`, `reasons`, `explanation`, **`deltas`**, `created_at` |
| 7 | `accumulators` | per-member/year running totals | `member_id`, `plan_year`, `dimension`, `used_cents`, `used_count` |
| 8 | `disputes` | challenge + re-adjudication | `id`, `line_item_id`, `original_adjudication_id`, `resolved_adjudication_id`, `reason`, `corrected`, `outcome`, `state`, `opened_at`, `resolved_at` |
| 9 | `status_transition` | append-only audit timeline | `entity_type`, `entity_id`, `from_status`, `to_status`, `actor`, `reason`, `seq`, `created_at` |

🔒 = PHI (store plain in v1, "encrypt-at-rest" documented as candidate — engine never reads them).

## 🧭 6 design calls to validate (where SQL must decide what the TS types don't)

1. **Unions → JSON text columns.** `cost_share`, `limit`, `corrected` stored as JSON (mirrors the discriminated unions 1:1; avoids the nullable-column sprawl your skill bans). *Recommend: JSON.*
2. **`adjudications.deltas`** (`deductible_inc/oop_inc/limit_inc`) stored on the row — **required** for the cycle-35 dispute net-out (`current − this line's original deltas`). *Recommend: yes.*
3. **`accumulators` = one row per dimension** (`DEDUCTIBLE` / `OOP` / `LIMIT:<service_code>`), per the plan — not wide columns. *Recommend: per-dimension rows.*
4. **`reasons` = JSON text array** on adjudications. *Recommend: JSON array.*
5. **IDs = prefixed text** (`clm_`, `li_`, `adj_`…) to match your builders, not autoincrement. *Recommend: text.*
6. **SQLite primitives:** money = `INTEGER` cents, booleans = `0/1`, dates = ISO `TEXT`. *Standard.*

**Two questions before I scaffold:**
1. Do you have the "done" 23–27 code somewhere I should pull, or are we building fresh? (I'll assume **fresh** if you don't point me elsewhere.)
2. Any redlines on the 9 tables or the 6 design calls above — or are they validated to proceed?

content.text:
✅ **Aggregation cycles 22–25 complete** — all green, typecheck + lint clean.

## 📊 What each cycle locked in

| # | Behavior | Rule | Result |
|---|---|---|---|
| 22 | all approved | every line `APPROVED`, no straddle | `APPROVED` |
| 23 | all denied | every line `DENIED` | `DENIED` |
| 24 | mix | some approved + some denied | `PARTIALLY_APPROVED` |
| 25 | straddle + approved | an `APPROVED` line carrying `LIMIT_EXCEEDED` | `PARTIALLY_APPROVED` |

## 🧠 The final logic (in plain words)

```ts
if (every line DENIED)              → "DENIED"
if (any DENIED || any straddled)    → "PARTIALLY_APPROVED"
otherwise                          → "APPROVED"
```

**Key insight (cycle 25):** a *straddled* line is still `APPROVED` — the plan paid up to the remaining dollar cap and pushed the shortfall to the member. It's not a denial, but it's not a *full* payout either, so it makes the whole claim **partial**. We detect it by `status === "APPROVED" && reasons.includes("LIMIT_EXCEEDED")`.

> Note: `UNDER_REVIEW` (any line `NEEDS_REVIEW`) is intentionally **not** handled yet — no test demands it until the dispute cycles (32–36). Staying minimal.

## ✔️ Verification

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| Aggregation suite | 🟢 4/4 |
| Full suite | 🟢 25/25 (no regressions) |
| `tsc --noEmit` | 🟢 clean |
| `biome check` | 🟢 clean |

## 📦 Uncommitted — ready as 4 red→green pairs

Everything is staged in the working tree only (per your "commit when asked" rule). When you want it, this is **8 commits** (test→feat × 4):

```
test: aggregate all-approved claim → APPROVED      feat: aggregateClaimStatus all-approved
test: aggregate all-denied claim → DENIED          feat: aggregate all-denied
test: aggregate mixed claim → PARTIALLY_APPROVED   feat: aggregate mixed
test: aggregate straddle → PARTIALLY_APPROVED      feat: aggregate straddle detection
```

**3 follow-ups to keep moving:**
1. Want me to **commit** these now as the 8-commit red→green chain (the graded evidence), or keep batching?
2. Ready to move to the **DB scaffolding** (schema + connection factory + skeletons) we validated — building fresh?
3. Should I log a **TRACK.md** row marking cycles 22–25 done before we switch phases?

3	The only file that mutates freely. Every agent updates it at session close. Keep it
4	honest and current — the next agent trusts it instead of re-deriving everything.
5	
6	## Current focus
7	
8	Framing + design **closed**. C3 adjudication behavior locked in
9	[`docs/adjudication-plan.md`](docs/adjudication-plan.md) (pipeline, cost-share math,
10	determinism, writeback). **Lifecycle strengthened this session:** dropped `PAID`/settle from v1
11	(#19 — claim ends at APPROVED/PARTIALLY_APPROVED/DENIED; dispute reopens → UNDER_REVIEW), and
12	added a **status-transition audit log** (#20 — one append-only table via a `setStatus()`
13	chokepoint, surfaced on `GET /claims/:id`). **Dispute is now locked first-class (Q4 resolved,
14	#21–#23):** corrected-facts re-adjudication, disputable from any terminal line, single-line
15	accumulator net-out, a 4-value outcome taxonomy, and a visible reason+outcome — spec + cycles
16	32–36 in [`docs/adjudication-plan.md`](docs/adjudication-plan.md). **TDD build order now 36
17	cycles** (28–31 = transition log; 32–36 = dispute). **Next: start TDD cycle 1
18	(`no rule → NO_COVERAGE`), test-first.** (Housekeeping: regenerate the 3 stale infographics.)
19	
20	## Current phase
21	
22	`03-design` complete → `04-coding` (TDD) next — first red test is cycle 1.
23	
24	## Open questions
25	
26	- [x] **Q1 — Prior auth modeling.** RESOLVED → boolean precondition; missing → `PRIOR_AUTH_REQUIRED`, payable 0. PPO reduce-to-50% penalty documented as a divergence, not built.
27	- [x] **Q2 — Out-of-network policy.** RESOLVED → in-network only for v1 (allowed == billed); OON/unlisted service → `NO_COVERAGE`. Network/metal-tier/family fields omitted (no math impact).
28	- [x] **Q3 — Accumulator period boundaries.** RESOLVED → fixed plan-year window keyed on the policy.
29	- [x] **Q5 — Duplicate handling.** RESOLVED → fingerprint (`member_id + service_code + service_date + billed_cents`) computed at **intake**; the duplicate *decision* is made at **adjudication** as a soft `DUPLICATE_LINE_ITEM` (payable 0). Not an intake reject.
30	- [x] **Q6 — NEEDS_REVIEW resolution path.** RESOLVED → prior-auth missing is now a clean `DENIED`, so it no longer routes to `NEEDS_REVIEW`. `NEEDS_REVIEW` is reached **only** via a dispute reopen and clears by immediate auto re-adjudication. No human-review state in v1.
31	- [x] **Q4 — Dispute resolution.** RESOLVED → first-class corrected-facts re-adjudication (decisions #21–#23): disputable from any terminal line, single-line accumulator net-out (`current − this line's original deltas`), 4-value outcome (`UPHELD | OVERTURNED | PARTIALLY_OVERTURNED | MODIFIED`), original preserved immutably, no reviewer queue.
32	
33	## Blocked
34	
35	Nothing blocked.

38	
39	| # | Date | Decision | Reason | Reversed? |
40	|---|------|----------|--------|-----------|
41	| 1 | 2026-06-18 | TypeScript (strict) + Node 20 + vitest | Static types encode the domain and catch money/null bugs at compile time; vitest is fast and test-first friendly. | No |
42	| 2 | 2026-06-18 | SQLite over Postgres | Reviewer clones and runs with zero DB setup; synchronous better-sqlite3 keeps adjudication deterministic. Postgres adds clone friction for no rubric gain. | No |
43	| 3 | 2026-06-18 | Typed config over a rules DSL | 80/20 call for a 48h budget: typed coverage-rule records are reviewable and type-checked; a DSL/engine is meta-machinery the assignment explicitly does not reward. | No |
44	| 4 | 2026-06-18 | Integer cents for all money | Float math on money is the single most common claims bug (coinsurance %, accumulation). Integers make math exact and tests trustworthy. | No |
45	| 5 | 2026-06-18 | Cost-share as a discriminated union (`full_coverage`\|`copay`\|`coinsurance`) | Real benefits (UHC/Aetna/Cigna/BCBS/ACA) use exactly one mechanism per service; union makes the model say it and the adjudicator an exhaustive switch. | No |
46	| 6 | 2026-06-18 | Unit-typed limits (`none`\|`dollars`\|`visits`) | The most common real limit is a visit/day cap, which a dollars-only field can't express; dollars still satisfies the brief's "$Y/yr". | No |
47	| 7 | 2026-06-18 | Prior-auth = clean denial; OON/network/metal/family omitted | Each stored field must trace to a real adjudication effect in a single-network, per-member, allowed==billed v1. | No |
48	| 8 | 2026-06-18 | Reimbursement model (plan → member); PAID via explicit settle action, gateway success assumed | Brief says "claims for reimbursement" and lists `paid` in the lifecycle. 49	| 9 | 2026-06-18 | Member = opaque `member_id` anchor; PII minimized/separated, encryption-at-rest candidate | Brief flags sensitive health data; engine adjudicates on `member_id → policy + accumulators` and never needs the name. One human persona (no auth/roles). | No |
50	| 10 | 2026-06-18 | `NEEDS_REVIEW` is a valid line state (locked); prior-auth *routing* to it is **PROVISIONAL** | State comes from infographic 04 + brief's "1 needs review". 51	| 11 | 2026-06-18 | Capture `diagnosis_code` + `provider` on the Claim as encrypted, non-adjudicated PHI | Brief names them as sensitive data; capturing is where we *demonstrate* the PHI stance. Revises the earlier omit-lean (Fork #2). | No |
52	| 12 | 2026-06-18 | Claim + LineItem attributes locked; 12-code closed service catalog; `service_date` at claim level; `units` per line; Adjudication `reasons[]` array | Matches the infographics. Unlisted `service_code` → `NO_COVERAGE`. `reasons[]` array revises the single-dominant-code stance. | No |
53	| 13 | 2026-06-18 | C2 intake = N1–N5; reject = HTTP 4xx, never persisted (no `REJECTED` state); member-existence = intake reject, policy-active = adjudication deny | Industry reject≠deny boundary (Stedi: once adjudicated, can only be denied). A reject never enters the system. | No |
54	| 14 | 2026-06-18 | Accumulator carries `deductible_met_cents` + `oop_met_cents` + per-service `limit_used` | Infographic 02 omitted `deductible_met`; the deductible draw at adjudication needs it. | No |
55	| 15 | 2026-06-19 | Prior-auth missing → **clean DENY** (`PRIOR_AUTH_REQUIRED`, payable 0), not `NEEDS_REVIEW` | No reviewer queue in scope → `NEEDS_REVIEW` would freeze claims forever. A deny is deterministic + fully explainable. Supersedes #10; resolves Q6. | No |
56	| 16 | 2026-06-19 | Adjudication outcomes are **decisions (HTTP 200)**, not errors; only malformed/identity input is 4xx | A denial carries a reason + explanation (the brief's "explain why"); an HTTP error explains nothing and breaks the "1 denied line" scenario. | No |
57	| 17 | 2026-06-19 | `prior_auth_present` **defaults to `true`** on input (absence = auth present) | Most services need no auth → frictionless common input; denial fires only on explicit `false`. Documented demo simplification. | No |
58	| 18 | 2026-06-19 | **C3 adjudication behavior locked** → `docs/adjudication-plan.md` (pipeline, cost-share math, determinism, accumulator writeback, 27-step TDD order) | The "big open" item. Engine is now planned in enough detail to test-drive. Built with System-Architect + DBA agents grounded in the repo docs. | No |
59	| 19 | 2026-06-19 | **No `PAID` state / settle in v1** — claim lifecycle ends at APPROVED/PARTIALLY_APPROVED/DENIED; line items at APPROVED/DENIED/NEEDS_REVIEW; dispute reopens → UNDER_REVIEW | The payable *amount* is computed + explained; the PAID *state* needs a settle trigger + gateway (out of scope). Don't model a status we can't truthfully transition. `paid` deferred to v2. Supersedes the PAID half of #8. | No |
60	| 20 | 2026-06-19 | **Status-transition audit log** — one append-only polymorphic table via a `setStatus()` chokepoint (4 sites: submit/adjudicated/aggregated/dispute), surfaced on `GET /claims/:id`; not event-sourced | Strengthens lifecycle tracking (a named rubric signal) with ~2 functions; status columns stay source of truth; injected `seq` keeps re-runs deterministic. Adds TDD cycles 28–31. Designed with the System/Solution Architect. | No |
61	| 21 | 2026-06-19 | **Dispute trigger = corrected member facts + current-rule binding** (`corrected{prior_auth_present?, service_code?, billed_cents?, units?}`); no corrected facts → `UPHELD`; no reviewer override | A deterministic engine re-running identical inputs is a no-op; a meaningful flip needs a changed input. 62	| 22 | 2026-06-19 | **Disputable from any terminal line** (APPROVED/partial/DENIED); **single-line re-adjudication with accumulator net-out** (`current − this line's original deltas`); no sibling/cross-claim cascade | Members dispute underpayments too. Adds TDD cycles 32–36. Confirmed with the user after a 3-agent stress-test. | No |
64	
65	## Domain research findings (2026-06-18)
66	
67	Source: 6-agent web research across UnitedHealthcare, Aetna, Cigna, BCBS SBCs + ACA/CMS;
68	synthesis in `ai-artifacts/02-domain-research/`. Key findings that shaped the model:
69	
70	- **Three cost-share modes, never a stack:** preventive = free (ACA, no deductible); office/
71	  specialist/urgent/ER = flat **copay** (deductible waived); imaging/surgery/hospital =
72	  **coinsurance after deductible**. ER's copay-then-coinsurance is the one common exception.
73	- **Visit/day limits dominate** (PT ~20–60/yr, home health ~60–120/yr, SNF ~25–180 days/yr);
74	  **dollar limits are rare** (chiro ~$1,500/yr, hearing aids, infertility). → limits need a unit.
75	- **Copay waives deductible but counts to OOP; coinsurance applies deductible first.** Kept as an
76	  explicit per-rule `applies_deductible` bool (exceptions exist) rather than derived.
77	- **Two "no"s:** explicit `excluded` (adult dental/vision) vs. no rule at all → `NO_COVERAGE`.
78	- **Dropped as no-math-impact in v1:** metal tier, HMO/PPO network, OON tier, family deductible,
79	  separate Rx deductible, referral gating.
80

87	| 2026-06-18 | Claude Code (Opus) | 01-framing → 02-domain-research | Researched claim intake (UHC/Cigna/Aetna + CMS-1500/837); **locked all artifacts + actors**; defined the C2 intake slice (N1–N5); adopted `NEEDS_REVIEW` + `dx_code`/`provider` PHI capture per the infographics; closed Q5. | `/to-prd` the intake slice; regenerate the 3 stale infographics; resolve Q4/Q6 when adjudication (C3) starts. |
88	| 2026-06-19 | Claude Code (Opus) | 03-design | Reviewed framing trajectory (adversarial panel: direction good, was over-framing). Ran System-Architect + DBA agents to **lock C3 adjudication behavior** → wrote `docs/adjudication-plan.md` (pipeline + math + determinism + 27-step TDD order). Resolved prior-auth (clean DENY, #15), decision-vs-error boundary (#16), `prior_auth_present` default true (#17); closed Q6. Propagated across PRD, decisions, domain-model. | **Start TDD cycle 1 (`no rule → NO_COVERAGE`), test-first.** Confirm Q4 before cycle 27. Save this session's JSONL to `ai-artifacts/03-design/`. |
89	| 2026-06-19 | Claude Code (Opus) | 03-design | Strengthened lifecycle tracking: **dropped `PAID`/settle from v1** (#19) and locked a **status-transition audit log** (#20, designed with the System/Solution Architect) → TDD now 31 cycles. Propagated across PRD, decisions, domain-model, adjudication-plan, visual-reference. | **Detail the dispute events** (what a dispute writes + its re-adjudication transitions, Q4), then start TDD cycle 1. Save this session's JSONL to `ai-artifacts/03-design/`. |
90	| 2026-06-19 | Claude Code (Opus) | 03-design | **Closed Q4 — dispute made first-class.** Ran 3 agents (repo audit + domain research + architecture stress-test); surfaced the determinism no-op + the accumulator delta-reversal bug; user confirmed 4 forks. Locked #21–#23; fixed the "reverse deltas" wording → net-out; added dispute spec + cycles 32–36 to adjudication-plan; propagated to decisions (#16). | **Start TDD cycle 1 (`no rule → NO_COVERAGE`), test-first.** Propagate dispute to domain-model + insurance-domain skill. Save this session's JSONL to `ai-artifacts/03-design/`. |
91	
92	## Notes for next agent
93	
94	- Scaffold lives at the repo root (`/insurance`), not in a nested `claims-system/` — the
95	  existing `.git` is at the root, so the root *is* the project.
96	- `project-docs/` holds the original assignment brief. Reference it; do not edit it.
97	- Nothing in `app/` yet — first code must arrive via a `/tdd-cycle`, test first.
98	- **The adjudication build is fully specced in [`docs/adjudication-plan.md`](docs/adjudication-plan.md)** — the **36-cycle** TDD order is the coding checklist. Cycles 1–25 are pure (no DB); 26–36 touch SQLite (28–31 = the status-transition log; 32–36 = the first-class dispute flow).
99	- Load the `insurance-domain` + `tdd-discipline` skills before the first cycle.
100	- Per the assignment, this design session's raw JSONL must be saved into `ai-artifacts/03-design/` (use `/end-session`).

# TRACK.md — live cross-session memory

The only file that mutates freely. Every agent updates it at session close. Keep it
honest and current — the next agent trusts it instead of re-deriving everything.

## Current focus

Framing + design **closed**. C3 adjudication behavior locked in
[`docs/adjudication-plan.md`](docs/adjudication-plan.md) (pipeline, cost-share math,
determinism, writeback). **Lifecycle strengthened this session:** dropped `PAID`/settle from v1
(#19 — claim ends at APPROVED/PARTIALLY_APPROVED/DENIED; dispute reopens → UNDER_REVIEW), and
added a **status-transition audit log** (#20 — one append-only table via a `setStatus()`
chokepoint, surfaced on `GET /claims/:id`). **Dispute is now locked first-class (Q4 resolved,
#21–#23):** corrected-facts re-adjudication, disputable from any terminal line, single-line
accumulator net-out, a 4-value outcome taxonomy, and a visible reason+outcome — spec + cycles
32–36 in [`docs/adjudication-plan.md`](docs/adjudication-plan.md). **TDD build order now 36
cycles** (28–31 = transition log; 32–36 = dispute). **Next: start TDD cycle 1
(`no rule → NO_COVERAGE`), test-first.** (Housekeeping: regenerate the 3 stale infographics.)

## Current phase

`03-design` complete → `04-coding` (TDD) next — first red test is cycle 1.

## Open questions

- [x] **Q1 — Prior auth modeling.** RESOLVED → boolean precondition; missing → `PRIOR_AUTH_REQUIRED`, payable 0. PPO reduce-to-50% penalty documented as a divergence, not built.
- [x] **Q2 — Out-of-network policy.** RESOLVED → in-network only for v1 (allowed == billed); OON/unlisted service → `NO_COVERAGE`. Network/metal-tier/family fields omitted (no math impact).

- [x] **Q4 — Dispute resolution.** RESOLVED → first-class corrected-facts re-adjudication (decisions #21–#23): disputable from any terminal line, single-line accumulator net-out (`current − this line's original deltas`), 4-value outcome (`UPHELD | OVERTURNED | PARTIALLY_OVERTURNED | MODIFIED`), original preserved immutably, no reviewer queue.

Ran System-Architect + DBA agents to **lock C3 adjudication behavior** → wrote `docs/adjudication-plan.md` (pipeline + math + determinism + 27-step TDD order). Resolved prior-auth (clean DENY, #15), decision-vs-error boundary (#16), `prior_auth_present` default true (#17); closed Q6. Propagated across PRD, decisions, domain-model. | **Start TDD cycle 1 (`no rule → NO_COVERAGE`), test-first.** Confirm Q4 before cycle 27. Save this session's JSONL to `ai-artifacts/03-design/`. |
| 2026-06-19 | Claude Code (Opus) | 03-design | Strengthened lifecycle tracking: **dropped `PAID`/settle from v1** (#19) and locked a **status-transition audit log** (#20, designed with the System/Solution Architect) → TDD now 31 cycles. Propagated across PRD, decisions, domain-model, adjudication-plan, visual-reference. | **Detail the dispute events** (what a dispute writes + its re-adjudication transitions, Q4), then start TDD cycle 1. Save this session's JSONL to `ai-artifacts/03-design/`. |
| 2026-06-19 | Claude Code (Opus) | 03-design | **Closed Q4 — dispute made first-class.** Ran 3 agents (repo audit + domain research + architecture stress-test); surfaced the determinism no-op + the accumulator delta-reversal bug; user confirmed 4 forks. Locked #21–#23; fixed the "reverse deltas" wording → net-out; added dispute spec + cycles 32–36 to adjudication-plan; propagated to decisions (#16). | **Start TDD cycle 1 (`no rule → NO_COVERAGE`), test-first.** Propagate dispute to domain-model + insurance-domain skill. Save this session's JSONL to `ai-artifacts/03-design/`. |

## Notes for next agent

- Scaffold lives at the repo root (`/insurance`), not in a nested `claims-system/` — the
  existing `.git` is at the root, so the root *is* the project.
- `project-docs/` holds the original assignment brief. Reference it; do not edit it.
- Nothing in `app/` yet — first code must arrive via a `/tdd-cycle`, test first.
- **The adjudication build is fully specced in [`docs/adjudication-plan.md`](docs/adjudication-plan.md)** — the **36-cycle** TDD order is the coding checklist. Cycles 1–25 are pure (no DB); 26–36 touch SQLite (28–31 = the status-transition log; 32–36 = the first-class dispute flow).
- Load the `insurance-domain` + `tdd-discipline` skills before the first cycle.
- Per the assignment, this design session's raw JSONL must be saved into `ai-artifacts/03-design/` (use `/end-session`).

content.content:
1	# Decisions & trade-offs
2	
3	> Status: template, pre-seeded from framing. Add a numbered entry whenever a real choice is
4	> made. Each entry: the decision, the alternative not taken, and the trade-off. This is the
5	> doc that shows engineering judgment — keep it honest.
6	
7	## Format
8	
9	`#N — Decision` → **Chose:** … **Over:** … **Trade-off:** … **Reversible?:** …
10	
11	---
12	
13	### 1 — Language and runtime: TypeScript (strict) + Node 20, vitest
14	
15	**Chose:** TypeScript strict mode on Node 20, tested with vitest.
16	**Over:** plain JS, or Python/FastAPI.
17	**Trade-off:** Strict types add upfront friction but encode the domain (money as branded
18	cents, exhaustive reason-code switches) and catch a class of bugs at compile time. vitest
19	keeps the red-green loop fast.
20	**Reversible?:** Low cost early, high cost later.
21	
22	### 2 — Persistence: SQLite (better-sqlite3) over Postgres
23	
24	**Chose:** SQLite via better-sqlite3.
25	**Over:** Postgres / Docker-compose stack.
26	**Trade-off:** A reviewer clones and runs with zero DB setup, and the synchronous driver
27	keeps adjudication deterministic and easy to test. Cost: no real concurrency story; fine
28	for a demo, called out as a limitation in self-review.
29	**Reversible?:** Moderate — schema is portable; the synchronous-driver assumption is not.
30	
31	### 3 — Rule representation: typed config over a DSL / rules engine
32	
33	**Chose:** Coverage rules as typed config records, applied by a fixed-order adjudicator.
34	**Over:** A rules DSL or a pluggable rules engine.
35	**Trade-off:** Typed config is reviewable, type-checked, and enough for the rules in scope.
36	A DSL is meta-machinery the assignment explicitly does not reward and would eat the 48h
37	budget. Cost: adding a fundamentally new *kind* of rule means code, not config.
38	**Reversible?:** Yes — the adjudicator is the seam; a DSL could be layered later.
39	
40	### 4 — Money: integer cents everywhere
41	
42	**Chose:** All monetary values as integer cents; percentages applied with explicit rounding
43	at the cents step.
44	**Over:** Floating-point dollars, or a decimal library.
45	**Trade-off:** Integers make money math exact and tests trustworthy — float math on money
46	(coinsurance %, accumulation) is the most common claims bug. Cost: must round consciously
47	where coinsurance produces fractional cents (member + plan shares must sum to allowed).
48	**Reversible?:** Hard — touches every amount; chosen deliberately up front.
49	
50	### 5 — Interface: REST API (fastify) over CLI or web UI
51	
52	**Chose:** Four REST endpoints via fastify.
53	**Over:** CLI, or a web UI.
54	**Trade-off:** REST is the clearest way to demonstrate the domain to a reviewer and maps
55	1:1 to the operations (submit, fetch, explain, dispute). A UI would burn budget on
56	non-rubric surface. _Confirm this stands after framing._
57	**Reversible?:** Yes — the domain core is interface-agnostic.
58	
59	### 6 — Cost-share: a discriminated union, not nullable copay + coinsurance fields
60	
61	**Chose:** `costShare: { type: "full_coverage" } | { type: "copay"; copayCents } | { type: "coinsurance"; rate }` — one mechanism per rule.
62	**Over:** a flat record with both `copayCents` and `coinsuranceRate` applied in sequence.
63	**Trade-off:** Research across UHC, Aetna, Cigna, BCBS, and ACA showed real benefits use
64	*one* mechanism — preventive is full coverage, office/ER/urgent are copay, imaging/surgery/
65	hospital are coinsurance after deductible. A union makes the model *say* that, turns the
66	adjudicator into an exhaustive switch, and rejects incoherent rules at the type level. Cost:
67	the rare copay-then-coinsurance case (ER) is approximated by its dominant component.
68	**Reversible?:** Yes — a 4th union variant can be added; the adjudicator switch is the seam.
69	
70	### 7 — Annual limit: unit-typed (`none` | `dollars` | `visits`), not dollars-only
71	
72	**Chose:** `limit: { unit: "none" } | { unit: "dollars"; amountCents } | { unit: "visits"; count }`,
73	with the accumulator's `limit_used` tracked in the same unit.
74	**Over:** a single `annualLimitCents` field.
75	**Trade-off:** The brief's one concrete example ("$Y per year") is a dollar limit — but the
76	*most common* real limit is a visit/day cap (PT 20 visits/yr, SNF 60 days/yr), which a
77	cents-only field literally cannot express. The unit discriminator handles both on one code
78	path. Cost: the accumulator and limit check become unit-aware (small). This is a deliberate
79	step beyond the brief's literal ask, justified by domain research.
80	**Reversible?:** Moderate — touches the accumulator's limit column and the limit step.
81	
82	### 8 — Prior-auth missing → clean DENY (RESOLVED in C3 brainstorm)
83	
84	> **Status: RESOLVED** (C3 brainstorm, this session). Supersedes the earlier provisional
85	> `NEEDS_REVIEW` lean.
86	
87	**Chose:** A prior-auth-required line with no auth present → a clean `DENIED` line carrying
88	`PRIOR_AUTH_REQUIRED`, payable 0, with an explanation. It is an adjudication **decision**
89	(HTTP 200), never an error.
90	**Over:** Routing it to `NEEDS_REVIEW` (the brief's *"1 needs review"* lean).
91	**Trade-off:** With no human-reviewer queue in scope, a `NEEDS_REVIEW` line would never
92	resolve and would freeze claim aggregation at `UNDER_REVIEW` forever. A clean deny is
93	deterministic and fully explainable — the brief's "explain why it was denied" is satisfied by
94	the reason code + sentence. Cost: we don't showcase a review-queue flow (out of scope anyway).
95	`NEEDS_REVIEW` survives as a state but is reached **only** via a dispute reopen, and clears by
96	immediate auto re-adjudication (Q4 lean). This resolves the old Q6.
97	**Reversible?:** Yes — prior-auth returns a single routing flag; swapping to `NEEDS_REVIEW`
98	is a one-line change if a queue is ever added.
99	
100	### 9 — Capture `diagnosis_code` + `provider` as encrypted, non-adjudicated PHI
101	
102	**Chose:** Capture `diagnosis_code` and `provider` on the Claim, store them as sensitive
103	(encryption-at-rest candidate), and **never** read them in adjudication.
104	**Over:** Omitting them entirely (the earlier research lean).
105	**Trade-off:** The brief explicitly names them as sensitive health data; capturing them is
106	where we *demonstrate* the PHI-handling the brief is testing. Cost: PHI we must protect for
107	zero math benefit — accepted deliberately, documented as the showcase of the PHI stance.
108	**Reversible?:** Yes — they're inert fields.
109	
110	### 10 — Claim + LineItem shape, closed 12-code catalog, `reasons[]` array
111	
112	**Chose:** Claim = `{ id, member_id, service_date (claim-level), provider, diagnosis_code,
113	status (derived) }`; LineItem = `{ id, claim_id, service_code, billed_cents, units,
114	prior_auth_present, status, fingerprint }`; a **closed 12-entry `service_code` catalog**;
115	Adjudication carries a `reasons[]` array.
116	**Over:** Per-line service dates; an open service vocabulary; a single dominant reason code.
117	**Trade-off:** Claim-level date + closed catalog keep v1 simple and make `NO_COVERAGE`
118	well-defined. `reasons[]` gives a richer EOB breakdown than one dominant code. Cost: can't
119	model a claim spanning multiple service dates (documented).
120	**Reversible?:** Moderate for the date; easy for the rest.
121	
122	### 11 — C2 intake: reject = HTTP 4xx, never persisted (no `REJECTED` state)
123	
124	**Chose:** Structural/identity failures (bad shape, non-integer cents, future/invalid date,
125	**unknown member**) → HTTP `4xx` with `{ errors: [{ field, code, message }] }`, nothing
126	persisted. Member *existence* is an intake reject; policy *active-on-date* is an
127	adjudication `POLICY_NOT_ACTIVE` deny.
128	**Over:** Persisting a `REJECTED` claim row for auditability.
129	**Trade-off:** Matches the industry reject≠deny boundary (a reject never enters the system,
130	no appeal) and avoids bloating the frozen claim state machine. Cost: no built-in audit trail
131	of bad submissions — if needed, log them separately, not as Claim rows.
132	**Reversible?:** Yes — a reject log can be added without touching the claim machine.
133	
134	### 12 — Adjudication outcomes are decisions (HTTP 200), not errors
135	
136	**Chose:** Every adjudication outcome — including all denials (`NO_COVERAGE`, `EXCLUDED`,
137	`PRIOR_AUTH_REQUIRED`, `LIMIT_EXCEEDED`, `POLICY_NOT_ACTIVE`, `DUPLICATE_LINE_ITEM`) — returns
138	HTTP `200` with `status` + `reason` + `explanation`. A denied line is a *processed* line.
139	Only malformed/identity-failed **input** is an HTTP `4xx` (the C2 intake reject, decision #11).
140	**Over:** Treating a business denial (e.g. prior-auth missing) as an HTTP error.
141	**Trade-off:** Errors explain nothing to a member and would break the brief's *"5 line items,
142	1 denied"* scenario and *"explain why it was denied."* A decision carries the explanation.
143	Cost: callers must read per-line status, not just the HTTP code — correct and intended.
144	**Reversible?:** N/A — this is the core contract of the engine.
145	
146	### 13 — `prior_auth_present` defaults to `true` on input (absence = auth present)
147	
148	**Chose:** When a line item omits `prior_auth_present`, treat it as `true`. The
149	`PRIOR_AUTH_REQUIRED` denial path fires only on an explicit `false`.
150	**Over:** Defaulting to `false` (absence = no auth).
151	**Trade-off:** Most services don't require prior auth, so a `true` default keeps the common
152	input frictionless; callers assert `false` only when relevant. Cost: a caller who forgets to
153	send `false` for an auth-required service is assumed to have had auth — an accepted demo
154	simplification, documented here. (Real systems treat absence as unauthorized.)
155	**Reversible?:** Yes — a one-line default change.
156	
157	### 14 — No `PAID` state / settle action in v1
158	
159	**Chose:** The claim lifecycle ends at `APPROVED` / `PARTIALLY_APPROVED` / `DENIED`; line items
160	at `APPROVED` / `DENIED` / `NEEDS_REVIEW`. No `PAID` state, no settle/pay endpoint. A dispute
161	reopens a terminal claim to `UNDER_REVIEW`.
162	**Over:** Modeling `paid` + an explicit settle action (the brief lists `paid` in the lifecycle).
163	**Trade-off:** The reimbursement *amount* (`payable_cents`, plan → member) is already computed
164	and explained. The `PAID` *state* only records that a disbursement happened — which needs a
165	settle trigger and a payment gateway, both out of scope. Modeling a status we can't truthfully
166	transition adds a dead state. `paid` is documented as a deferred v2 transition; the
167	status-transition log already has the slot for it. Cost: we don't demonstrate the full
168	submitted→paid arc.
169	**Reversible?:** Yes — add a settle action + `PAID` state later; the transition log is ready.
170	
171	### 15 — Status-transition audit log (one append-only table, not event-sourced)
172	
173	**Chose:** One shared polymorphic `status_transition` table (`entity_type`, `entity_id`,
174	`from`, `to`, `actor`, `reason`, `seq`, `created_at`) appended via a single `setStatus()`
175	chokepoint at 4 sites (submit · adjudicated · aggregated · dispute reopen); surfaced as a
176	`timeline` on `GET /claims/:id`.
177	**Over:** Two per-entity tables; or full event sourcing (replaying the log to derive state).
178	**Trade-off:** Directly serves the brief's *"state machine of a claim vs. line item"* +
179	*"track through lifecycle"* with ~2 small functions. Status columns stay the source of truth;
180	the log is a parallel audit trail. `actor` is a `SYSTEM | MEMBER` enum (no auth/user entity).
181	`seq` is an injected logical clock so re-runs stay deterministic; `created_at` is metadata
182	never read by logic/tests. Cost: a small write at each transition; adds TDD cycles 28–31.
183	**Reversible?:** Yes — additive; the table can be dropped without touching adjudication.
184	
185	### 16 — Dispute is a first-class flow (Q4 resolved)
186	
187	**Chose:** A dispute is a member-initiated, **synchronous re-adjudication** of one line item that
188	carries **corrected facts**, preserves the original decision immutably, and resolves to a
189	**4-value outcome**.
190	- **Trigger — corrected facts, not a pure re-run.** The dispute body is `{ reason, corrected? }`
191	  where `corrected` may amend only `{ prior_auth_present, service_code, billed_cents, units }`. A
192	  deterministic engine re-running identical inputs is a no-op; a meaningful flip needs a changed
193	  input. Re-adjudication binds to **current** coverage rules, so a retroactive rule change also
194	  flips an outcome for free. No corrected facts → an honest `UPHELD`.
195	- **Disputable from any terminal line** (`APPROVED` / partial / `DENIED`), not denial-only — real
196	  members dispute underpayments and miscomputed cost-share, not just outright denials.
197	- **Single-line scope with accumulator net-out.** Re-adjudicate only the disputed line against
198	  `current accumulator − that line's original deltas`, then apply the new deltas. Invariant: each
199	  accumulator dimension = Σ of every line's *latest* adjudication deltas. Sibling / cross-claim
200	  lines are **not** cascaded.
201	- **4-value outcome** `UPHELD | OVERTURNED | PARTIALLY_OVERTURNED | MODIFIED`, computed by diffing
202	  the new vs original adjudication (status / payable / reasons). Line transitions
203	  `{APPROVED|DENIED} → NEEDS_REVIEW → {APPROVED|DENIED}`; claim `terminal → UNDER_REVIEW →
204	  terminal`; dispute `OPEN → RESOLVED`.
205	
206	**Over:** (a) a pure no-op re-run (theatre — never flips); (b) a human reviewer queue /
207	discretionary `DISPUTED_OVERRIDE` (no reviewer actor in v1 scope); (c) blind delta-reversal on
208	re-adjudication (corrupts shared accumulators — double-counts the deductible or drives OOP negative
209	once later claims have advanced them); (d) whole-claim re-adjudication (re-touches undisputed lines,
210	bloats append-only history).
211	**Trade-off:** Corrected-facts re-adjudication reuses the entire deterministic engine unchanged and
212	demonstrates the most common real dispute (a supplied prior-auth number flips `PRIOR_AUTH_REQUIRED`
213	→ `APPROVED`; OIG found MA plans overturn ~75% of appealed denials, mostly on corrected info). Cost:
214	single-line scope means an intervening line decided against the pre-dispute accumulator is **not**
215	recomputed — a documented v1 limitation, not a cross-claim recompute engine. `DISPUTED_OVERRIDE`
216	stays in the enum as the v2 reviewer-override slot.
217	**Reversible?:** Yes — `corrected{}` is additive; a reviewer/override path and whole-claim cascade
218	can layer on later.
219	
220	---
221	
222	## Decisions resolved this framing session
223	
224	- **Prior-auth modeling** (Q1) — boolean precondition; missing → `PRIOR_AUTH_REQUIRED`, payable 0. PPO reduce-to-50% penalty documented as a divergence, not built.
225	- **Out-of-network** (Q2) — in-network only for v1 (allowed == billed); OON/unlisted service → `NO_COVERAGE`. Network/metal/family fields omitted.
226	- **Accumulator period** (Q3) — fixed plan-year window keyed on the policy.
227	
228	## Decisions still open (resolve and move up)
229	
230	- _None._ All framing/design questions resolved; ready to test-drive.
231	
232	## Decisions resolved (moved up)
233	
234	- **Dispute resolution (Q4)** — RESOLVED: first-class corrected-facts re-adjudication, disputable
235	  from any terminal line, single-line net-out accumulators, 4-value outcome, no reviewer queue.
236	  See decision #16.
237	- **Duplicate handling (Q5)** — RESOLVED: fingerprint computed at intake; soft `DUPLICATE_LINE_ITEM` decided at adjudication. See decision #11 / TRACK #12.
238	- **C3 adjudication behavior** — RESOLVED (this session): full pipeline, cost-share math,
239	  determinism, accumulator writeback, and the TDD build order are planned in
240	  [`docs/adjudication-plan.md`](adjudication-plan.md). Ready to test-drive.
241	- **Prior-auth routing (was Q6 / decision #8)** — RESOLVED: clean DENY, not `NEEDS_REVIEW`.
242	  See decision #8.
243	
244	## Assumptions about the domain
245	
246	1. Single currency (USD), integer cents.
247	2. One policy per member per plan year; no coordination of benefits.
248	3. Allowed amount == billed amount (no fee schedule lookup).
249	4. Plan year is a fixed window on the policy; accumulators align to it.
250	5. Prior auth is a recorded precondition, not a workflow.
251	6. Determinism over wall-clock; concurrency beyond SQLite's single-writer is out of scope.
252	7. One cost-share mechanism per service; copay-then-coinsurance approximated by dominant component.
253	8. Limits are unit-typed (`dollars` or `visits`); supply-window/replacement-frequency limits out of scope.
254	9. `prior_auth_present` defaults to `true` on input (absence = auth present); denial fires only on explicit `false`.
255	10. Adjudication denials are decisions (HTTP 200 + reason + explanation); only malformed/identity input is HTTP 4xx.
256	11. No `PAID` state / settle in v1; `paid` is a deferred v2 transition (decision #14).
257	12. Every status change is recorded in an append-only status-transition log, not event-sourced (decision #15).
258	13. A dispute carries corrected facts (`prior_auth_present`/`service_code`/`billed_cents`/`units`) and re-adjudicates against current rules; absent corrections → `UPHELD` (decision #16).
259	14. Dispute re-adjudication is single-line and nets out the line's own prior deltas; intervening sibling / cross-claim lines are not cascaded (decision #16).
260	15. No human reviewer queue; `DISPUTED_OVERRIDE` is reserved for a v2 reviewer-override path (decision #16).
261	
262	_Add to this list whenever the build forces a judgment call the assignment left open._
263

# Decisions & trade-offs

> Status: template, pre-seeded from framing. Add a numbered entry whenever a real choice is
> made. Each entry: the decision, the alternative not taken, and the trade-off. This is the
> doc that shows engineering judgment — keep it honest.

## Format

`#N — Decision` → **Chose:** … **Over:** … **Trade-off:** … **Reversible?:** …

---

### 1 — Language and runtime: TypeScript (strict) + Node 20, vitest

**Chose:** TypeScript strict mode on Node 20, tested with vitest.
**Over:** plain JS, or Python/FastAPI.
**Trade-off:** Strict types add upfront friction but encode the domain (money as branded
cents, exhaustive reason-code switches) and catch a class of bugs at compile time. vitest
keeps the red-green loop fast.
**Reversible?:** Low cost early, high cost later.

### 2 — Persistence: SQLite (better-sqlite3) over Postgres

**Chose:** SQLite via better-sqlite3.
**Over:** Postgres / Docker-compose stack.
**Trade-off:** A reviewer clones and runs with zero DB setup, and the synchronous driver
keeps adjudication deterministic and easy to test. Cost: no real concurrency story; fine
for a demo, called out as a limitation in self-review.
**Reversible?:** Moderate — schema is portable; the synchronous-driver assumption is not.

### 3 — Rule representation: typed config over a DSL / rules engine

**Chose:** Coverage rules as typed config records, applied by a fixed-order adjudicator.
**Over:** A rules DSL or a pluggable rules engine.
**Trade-off:** Typed config is reviewable, type-checked, and enough for the rules in scope.
A DSL is meta-machinery the assignment explicitly does not reward and would eat the 48h
budget. Cost: adding a fundamentally new *kind* of rule means code, not config.
**Reversible?:** Yes — the adjudicator is the seam; a DSL could be layered later.

### 4 — Money: integer cents everywhere

**Chose:** All monetary values as integer cents; percentages applied with explicit rounding
at the cents step.
**Over:** Floating-point dollars, or a decimal library.
**Trade-off:** Integers make money math exact and tests trustworthy — float math on money
(coinsurance %, accumulation) is the most common claims bug. Cost: must round consciously
where coinsurance produces fractional cents (member + plan shares must sum to allowed).
**Reversible?:** Hard — touches every amount; chosen deliberately up front.

### 5 — Interface: REST API (fastify) over CLI or web UI

**Chose:** Four REST endpoints via fastify.
**Over:** CLI, or a web UI.
**Trade-off:** REST is the clearest way to demonstrate the domain to a reviewer and maps
1:1 to the operations (submit, fetch, explain, dispute). A UI would burn budget on
non-rubric surface. _Confirm this stands after framing._
**Reversible?:** Yes — the domain core is interface-agnostic.

### 6 — Cost-share: a discriminated union, not nullable copay + coinsurance fields

**Chose:** `costShare: { type: "full_coverage" } | { type: "copay"; copayCents } | { type: "coinsurance"; rate }` — one mechanism per rule.
**Over:** a flat record with both `copayCents` and `coinsuranceRate` applied in sequence.
**Trade-off:** Research across UHC, Aetna, Cigna, BCBS, and ACA showed real benefits use
*one* mechanism — preventive is full coverage, office/ER/urgent are copay, imaging/surgery/
hospital are coinsurance after deductible. A union makes the model *say* that, turns the
adjudicator into an exhaustive switch, and rejects incoherent rules at the type level. Cost:
the rare copay-then-coinsurance case (ER) is approximated by its dominant component.
**Reversible?:** Yes — a 4th union variant can be added; the adjudicator switch is the seam.

### 7 — Annual limit: unit-typed (`none` | `dollars` | `visits`), not dollars-only

**Chose:** `limit: { unit: "none" } | { unit: "dollars"; amountCents } | { unit: "visits"; count }`,
with the accumulator's `limit_used` tracked in the same unit.
**Over:** a single `annualLimitCents` field.
**Trade-off:** The brief's one concrete example ("$Y per year") is a dollar limit — but the
*most common* real limit is a visit/day cap (PT 20 visits/yr, SNF 60 days/yr), which a
cents-only field literally cannot express. The unit discriminator handles both on one code
path. Cost: the accumulator and limit check become unit-aware (small). This is a deliberate
step beyond the brief's literal ask, justified by domain research.
**Reversible?:** Moderate — touches the accumulator's limit column and the limit step.

### 8 — Prior-auth missing → clean DENY (RESOLVED in C3 brainstorm)

> **Status: RESOLVED** (C3 brainstorm, this session). Supersedes the earlier provisional
> `NEEDS_REVIEW` lean.

**Chose:** A prior-auth-required line with no auth present → a clean `DENIED` line carrying
`PRIOR_AUTH_REQUIRED`, payable 0, with an explanation. It is an adjudication **decision**
(HTTP 200), never an error.
**Over:** Routing it to `NEEDS_REVIEW` (the brief's *"1 needs review"* lean).
**Trade-off:** With no human-reviewer queue in scope, a `NEEDS_REVIEW` line would never
resolve and would freeze claim aggregation at `UNDER_REVIEW` forever. A clean deny is
deterministic and fully explainable — the brief's "explain why it was denied" is satisfied by
the reason code + sentence. Cost: we don't showcase a review-queue flow (out of scope anyway).
`NEEDS_REVIEW` survives as a state but is reached **only** via a dispute reopen, and clears by
immediate auto re-adjudication (Q4 lean). This resolves the old Q6.
**Reversible?:** Yes — prior-auth returns a single routing flag; swapping to `NEEDS_REVIEW`
is a one-line change if a queue is ever added.

### 9 — Capture `diagnosis_code` + `provider` as encrypted, non-adjudicated PHI

**Chose:** Capture `diagnosis_code` and `provider` on the Claim, store them as sensitive
(encryption-at-rest candidate), and **never** read them in adjudication.
**Over:** Omitting them entirely (the earlier research lean).
**Trade-off:** The brief explicitly names them as sensitive health data; capturing them is
where we *demonstrate* the PHI-handling the brief is testing. Cost: PHI we must protect for
zero math benefit — accepted deliberately, documented as the showcase of the PHI stance.
**Reversible?:** Yes — they're inert fields.

### 10 — Claim + LineItem shape, closed 12-code catalog, `reasons[]` array

**Chose:** Claim = `{ id, member_id, service_date (claim-level), provider, diagnosis_code,
status (derived) }`; LineItem = `{ id, claim_id, service_code, billed_cents, units,
prior_auth_present, status, fingerprint }`; a **closed 12-entry `service_code` catalog**;
Adjudication carries a `reasons[]` array.
**Over:** Per-line service dates; an open service vocabulary; a single dominant reason code.
**Trade-off:** Claim-level date + closed catalog keep v1 simple and make `NO_COVERAGE`
well-defined. `reasons[]` gives a richer EOB breakdown than one dominant code. Cost: can't
model a claim spanning multiple service dates (documented).
**Reversible?:** Moderate for the date; easy for the rest.

### 11 — C2 intake: reject = HTTP 4xx, never persisted (no `REJECTED` state)

**Chose:** Structural/identity failures (bad shape, non-integer cents, future/invalid date,
**unknown member**) → HTTP `4xx` with `{ errors: [{ field, code, message }] }`, nothing
persisted. Member *existence* is an intake reject; policy *active-on-date* is an
adjudication `POLICY_NOT_ACTIVE` deny.
**Over:** Persisting a `REJECTED` claim row for auditability.
**Trade-off:** Matches the industry reject≠deny boundary (a reject never enters the system,
no appeal) and avoids bloating the frozen claim state machine. Cost: no built-in audit trail
of bad submissions — if needed, log them separately, not as Claim rows.
**Reversible?:** Yes — a reject log can be added without touching the claim machine.

### 12 — Adjudication outcomes are decisions (HTTP 200), not errors

**Chose:** Every adjudication outcome — including all denials (`NO_COVERAGE`, `EXCLUDED`,
`PRIOR_AUTH_REQUIRED`, `LIMIT_EXCEEDED`, `POLICY_NOT_ACTIVE`, `DUPLICATE_LINE_ITEM`) — returns
HTTP `200` with `status` + `reason` + `explanation`. A denied line is a *processed* line.
Only malformed/identity-failed **input** is an HTTP `4xx` (the C2 intake reject, decision #11).
**Over:** Treating a business denial (e.g. prior-auth missing) as an HTTP error.
**Trade-off:** Errors explain nothing to a member and would break the brief's *"5 line items,
1 denied"* scenario and *"explain why it was denied."* A decision carries the explanation.
Cost: callers must read per-line status, not just the HTTP code — correct and intended.
**Reversible?:** N/A — this is the core contract of the engine.

### 13 — `prior_auth_present` defaults to `true` on input (absence = auth present)

**Chose:** When a line item omits `prior_auth_present`, treat it as `true`. The
`PRIOR_AUTH_REQUIRED` denial path fires only on an explicit `false`.
**Over:** Defaulting to `false` (absence = no auth).
**Trade-off:** Most services don't require prior auth, so a `true` default keeps the common
input frictionless; callers assert `false` only when relevant. Cost: a caller who forgets to
send `false` for an auth-required service is assumed to have had auth — an accepted demo
simplification, documented here. (Real systems treat absence as unauthorized.)
**Reversible?:** Yes — a one-line default change.

### 14 — No `PAID` state / settle action in v1

**Chose:** The claim lifecycle ends at `APPROVED` / `PARTIALLY_APPROVED` / `DENIED`; line items
at `APPROVED` / `DENIED` / `NEEDS_REVIEW`. No `PAID` state, no settle/pay endpoint. A dispute
reopens a terminal claim to `UNDER_REVIEW`.
**Over:** Modeling `paid` + an explicit settle action (the brief lists `paid` in the lifecycle).
**Trade-off:** The reimbursement *amount* (`payable_cents`, plan → member) is already computed
and explained. The `PAID` *state* only records that a disbursement happened — which needs a
settle trigger and a payment gateway, both out of scope. Modeling a status we can't truthfully
transition adds a dead state. `paid` is documented as a deferred v2 transition; the
status-transition log already has the slot for it. Cost: we don't demonstrate the full
submitted→paid arc.
**Reversible?:** Yes — add a settle action + `PAID` state later; the transition log is ready.

### 15 — Status-transition audit log (one append-only table, not event-sourced)

**Chose:** One shared polymorphic `status_transition` table (`entity_type`, `entity_id`,
`from`, `to`, `actor`, `reason`, `seq`, `created_at`) appended via a single `setStatus()`
chokepoint at 4 sites (submit · adjudicated · aggregated · dispute reopen); surfaced as a
`timeline` on `GET /claims/:id`.
**Over:** Two per-entity tables; or full event sourcing (replaying the log to derive state).
**Trade-off:** Directly serves the brief's *"state machine of a claim vs. line item"* +
*"track through lifecycle"* with ~2 small functions. Status columns stay the source of truth;
the log is a parallel audit trail. `actor` is a `SYSTEM | MEMBER` enum (no auth/user entity).
`seq` is an injected logical clock so re-runs stay deterministic; `created_at` is metadata
never read by logic/tests. Cost: a small write at each transition; adds TDD cycles 28–31.
**Reversible?:** Yes — additive; the table can be dropped without touching adjudication.

### 16 — Dispute is a first-class flow (Q4 resolved)

**Chose:** A dispute is a member-initiated, **synchronous re-adjudication** of one line item that
carries **corrected facts**, preserves the original decision immutably, and resolves to a
**4-value outcome**.
- **Trigger — corrected facts, not a pure re-run.** The dispute body is `{ reason, corrected? }`
  where `corrected` may amend only `{ prior_auth_present, service_code, billed_cents, units }`. A
  deterministic engine re-running identical inputs is a no-op; a meaningful flip needs a changed
  input. Re-adjudication binds to **current** coverage rules, so a retroactive rule change also
  flips an outcome for free. No corrected facts → an honest `UPHELD`.
- **Disputable from any terminal line** (`APPROVED` / partial / `DENIED`), not denial-only — real
  members dispute underpayments and miscomputed cost-share, not just outright denials.
- **Single-line scope with accumulator net-out.** Re-adjudicate only the disputed line against
  `current accumulator − that line's original deltas`, then apply the new deltas. Invariant: each
  accumulator dimension = Σ of every line's *latest* adjudication deltas. Sibling / cross-claim
  lines are **not** cascaded.
- **4-value outcome** `UPHELD | OVERTURNED | PARTIALLY_OVERTURNED | MODIFIED`, computed by diffing
  the new vs original adjudication (status / payable / reasons). Line transitions
  `{APPROVED|DENIED} → NEEDS_REVIEW → {APPROVED|DENIED}`; claim `terminal → UNDER_REVIEW →
  terminal`; dispute `OPEN → RESOLVED`.

**Over:** (a) a pure no-op re-run (theatre — never flips); (b) a human reviewer queue /
discretionary `DISPUTED_OVERRIDE` (no reviewer actor in v1 scope); (c) blind delta-reversal on
re-adjudication (corrupts shared accumulators — double-counts the deductible or drives OOP negative
once later claims have advanced them); (d) whole-claim re-adjudication (re-touches undisputed lines,
bloats append-only history).
**Trade-off:** Corrected-facts re-adjudication reuses the entire deterministic engine unchanged and
demonstrates the most common real dispute (a supplied prior-auth number flips `PRIOR_AUTH_REQUIRED`
→ `APPROVED`; OIG found MA plans overturn ~75% of appealed denials, mostly on corrected info). Cost:
single-line scope means an intervening line decided against the pre-dispute accumulator is **not**
recomputed — a documented v1 limitation, not a cross-claim recompute engine. `DISPUTED_OVERRIDE`
stays in the enum as the v2 reviewer-override slot.
**Reversible?:** Yes — `corrected{}` is additive; a reviewer/override path and whole-claim cascade
can layer on later.

---

## Decisions resolved this framing session

- **Prior-auth modeling** (Q1) — boolean precondition; missing → `PRIOR_AUTH_REQUIRED`, payable 0. PPO reduce-to-50% penalty documented as a divergence, not built.
- **Out-of-network** (Q2) — in-network only for v1 (allowed == billed); OON/unlisted service → `NO_COVERAGE`. Network/metal/family fields omitted.
- **Accumulator period** (Q3) — fixed plan-year window keyed on the policy.

## Decisions still open (resolve and move up)

- _None._ All framing/design questions resolved; ready to test-drive.

## Decisions resolved (moved up)

- **Dispute resolution (Q4)** — RESOLVED: first-class corrected-facts re-adjudication, disputable
  from any terminal line, single-line net-out accumulators, 4-value outcome, no reviewer queue.
  See decision #16.
- **Duplicate handling (Q5)** — RESOLVED: fingerprint computed at intake; soft `DUPLICATE_LINE_ITEM` decided at adjudication. See decision #11 / TRACK #12.
- **C3 adjudication behavior** — RESOLVED (this session): full pipeline, cost-share math,
  determinism, accumulator writeback, and the TDD build order are planned in
  [`docs/adjudication-plan.md`](adjudication-plan.md). Ready to test-drive.
- **Prior-auth routing (was Q6 / decision #8)** — RESOLVED: clean DENY, not `NEEDS_REVIEW`.
  See decision #8.

## Assumptions about the domain

1. Single currency (USD), integer cents.
2. One policy per member per plan year; no coordination of benefits.
3. Allowed amount == billed amount (no fee schedule lookup).
4. Plan year is a fixed window on the policy; accumulators align to it.
5. Prior auth is a recorded precondition, not a workflow.
6. Determinism over wall-clock; concurrency beyond SQLite's single-writer is out of scope.
7. One cost-share mechanism per service; copay-then-coinsurance approximated by dominant component.
8. Limits are unit-typed (`dollars` or `visits`); supply-window/replacement-frequency limits out of scope.
9. `prior_auth_present` defaults to `true` on input (absence = auth present); denial fires only on explicit `false`.
10. Adjudication denials are decisions (HTTP 200 + reason + explanation); only malformed/identity input is HTTP 4xx.
11. No `PAID` state / settle in v1; `paid` is a deferred v2 transition (decision #14).
12. Every status change is recorded in an append-only status-transition log, not event-sourced (decision #15).
13. A dispute carries corrected facts (`prior_auth_present`/`service_code`/`billed_cents`/`units`) and re-adjudicates against current rules; absent corrections → `UPHELD` (decision #16).
14. Dispute re-adjudication is single-line and nets out the line's own prior deltas; intervening sibling / cross-claim lines are not cascaded (decision #16).
15. No human reviewer queue; `DISPUTED_OVERRIDE` is reserved for a v2 reviewer-override path (decision #16).

_Add to this list whenever the build forces a judgment call the assignment left open._

=== adjudicate-line.test.ts — it() descriptions ===
denies the line with NO_COVERAGE and pays nothing when no rule matches the service"
denies the line with EXCLUDED and pays nothing when the rule excludes the service"
denies with NO_COVERAGE when a rule exists but covered is false"
denies with POLICY_NOT_ACTIVE when the service date is outside the policy window"
cleanly denies with PRIOR_AUTH_REQUIRED when auth is required but not present"
denies with DUPLICATE_LINE_ITEM when the fingerprint was already adjudicated"
pays the plan 100% and the member nothing for a full-coverage service"
charges the flat copay and pays the rest when the allowed amount exceeds the copay"
caps the member charge at the allowed amount when the copay exceeds it (min clamp)"
charges the coinsurance rate on the full allowed when the deductible is already met"
sends the whole amount to the deductible (coinsurance 0, plan 0) when allowed is below the remaining deductible"
splits a deductible-crossing line into deductible draw + coinsurance on the remainder"
rounds the coinsurance share half-up and never loses a cent (member + plan === allowed)"
approves a visit and consumes one unit when the visit cap is not yet reached"
denies the whole visit with LIMIT_EXCEEDED once the visit cap is reached"
approves within the dollar cap and accrues the plan pay toward the limit"
straddles the dollar cap: plan caps at the remaining, the shortfall falls to the member, line stays APPROVED"
caps the member at the OOP max and refunds the excess to the plan"
pays the line 100% with the member owing nothing once the OOP max is already met"
line 2 sees line 1's deductible draw when the caller applies deltas between lines"
re-running the same line against the same snapshot yields identical results"

21

34

  deductible draw + half-up rounding), limits (visit cap + dollar straddle), OOP cap, and
  cross-line determinism (line 2 sees line 1's deltas; re-run is identical).

  `LineOutcome`; the `UNDER_REVIEW` branch is deliberately deferred to the dispute cycles (#24).

net-out, per-dimension accumulator rows, JSON `reasons[]`, prefixed-text ids, integer-cents /
bool / ISO-text primitives), and a **`createDb(path=':memory:')` connection factory** so each DB
test gets an isolated in-memory SQLite. Build as `chore:` scaffolding (no behavior), then cycles
**26–36** test-first (26–27 writeback, 28–31 transition log, 32–36 disputes).

## Decisions resolved this framing session
input.new_string:
**Reversible?:** Yes — `corrected{}` is additive; a reviewer/override path and whole-claim cascade
can layer on later.

### 17 — Claim aggregation: derive `PARTIALLY_APPROVED` from a line's reason code, not a partial line state

> **Status: IMPLEMENTED** (cycles 22–25, this session — `aggregateClaimStatus`).

**Chose:** `aggregateClaimStatus(lines)` derives the claim status from line outcomes — every line
`DENIED` → `DENIED`; **any** `DENIED` **or** any `APPROVED` line carrying `LIMIT_EXCEEDED` (a
dollar-limit straddle) → `PARTIALLY_APPROVED`; otherwise `APPROVED`. It reads a minimal
`LineOutcome = { status, reasons }`. The `UNDER_REVIEW` branch (any `NEEDS_REVIEW` line) is
deferred until the dispute cycles.
**Over:** (a) a partial / `PARTIALLY_APPROVED` **line** state; (b) threading a separate `isPartial`
flag out of the adjudicator; (c) passing the full adjudication result into the aggregator.
**Trade-off:** A straddle already records `LIMIT_EXCEEDED` on an otherwise-`APPROVED` line, so the
reason code is a faithful partial-payout signal — no new state, and `PARTIALLY_APPROVED` stays
**claim-level-only** (the locked line/claim split, decisions #14/#19). The minimal `LineOutcome`
keeps the pure aggregator decoupled from the persistence/result shape. Deferring `UNDER_REVIEW`
follows TDD minimalism — no test demands it until disputes (cycles 32–36). Cost: aggregation
depends on the adjudicator emitting `LIMIT_EXCEEDED` on a straddle (covered by cycles 17 + 25).
**Reversible?:** Yes — additive; the `UNDER_REVIEW` branch is a one-line add when disputes need it.

---

## Decisions resolved this framing session

content.content:
<system-reminder>[Truncated: PARTIAL view — showing lines 1-634 of 836 total (28000 tokens, cap 25000). Call Read with offset=635 limit=634 for the next page, or Grep to find a specific section. Do NOT answer from this page alone if the answer may be further in the file.]</system-reminder>

1	# Physical Database ERD — Claims Adjudication (SQLite)
2	
3	> Source of truth: `docs/domain-model.md`, `docs/adjudication-plan.md`, `docs/decisions.md`, `PRD.md`. Dialect: **SQLite via better-sqlite3 + Drizzle** (locked by decision #2; confirmed by `app/src/db/connection.ts` and `app/drizzle.config.ts` stub comments). This **physical** ERD supersedes the logical `docs/erd.md` without regressing any of its 9 entities. If docs and code ever drift, **the code wins** and this doc is updated.
4	
5	This revision incorporates every valid review finding. Where reviewers conflicted with a locked decision (most notably `service_code` free-text vs. the closed 12-entry catalog), the locked decision wins and the conflict is documented in **Design decisions & rationale**.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Entity-Relationship Diagram
10	
11	```mermaid
12	erDiagram
13	    MEMBERS ||--|| POLICIES : "has 1 per plan_year"
14	    MEMBERS ||--o{ CLAIMS : "submits"
15	    MEMBERS ||--o{ ACCUMULATORS : "tracked by (1+ rows/plan_year)"
16	    POLICIES ||--o{ COVERAGE_RULES : "defines"
17	    POLICIES ||--o{ CLAIMS : "adjudicated under (recorded policy_id)"
18	    CLAIMS ||--o{ LINE_ITEMS : "contains 1+"
19	    LINE_ITEMS ||--o{ ADJUDICATIONS : "decided by (append-only; latest seq = current)"
20	    LINE_ITEMS ||--o{ DISPUTES : "challenged by (terminal lines only)"
21	    ADJUDICATIONS ||--o{ DISPUTES : "challenged/resolved (see note: two FKs, one edge)"
22	    CLAIMS ||--o{ STATUS_TRANSITIONS : "logged via claim_id (polymorphic, exactly-one)"
23	    LINE_ITEMS ||--o{ STATUS_TRANSITIONS : "logged via line_item_id (polymorphic, exactly-one)"
24	
25	    MEMBERS {
26	        TEXT id PK "UUID surrogate"
27	        TEXT name "PHI; app-level encrypt-at-rest (SQLCipher); engine never reads"
28	        TEXT dob "PHI; ISO-8601 date; app-level encrypt; not adjudicated"
29	        TEXT created_at "ISO-8601 metadata"
30	    }
31	
32	    POLICIES {
33	        TEXT id PK "UUID surrogate"
34	        TEXT member_id FK "to members.id; RESTRICT del / CASCADE upd"
35	        TEXT plan_year "e.g. 2025; accumulator window key"
36	        TEXT effective_date "ISO-8601 date; GLOB-validated; policy-active lower bound"
37	        TEXT termination_date "ISO-8601 date; GLOB-validated; policy-active upper bound"
38	        INTEGER deductible_cents "cents; CHECK >= 0"
39	        INTEGER oop_max_cents "cents; CHECK >= 0; CHECK >= deductible_cents"
40	        TEXT created_at "ISO-8601 metadata"
41	        UK member_plan_year "UNIQUE(member_id, plan_year): 1 policy/member/year"
42	    }
43	
44	    COVERAGE_RULES {
45	        TEXT id PK "UUID surrogate"
46	        TEXT policy_id FK "to policies.id; CASCADE del / CASCADE upd"
47	        TEXT service_code "CHECK in closed 12-entry catalog (decision #10)"
48	        INTEGER covered "BOOL 0/1; CHECK IN (0,1)"
49	        INTEGER excluded "BOOL 0/1; excluded beats covered; CHECK IN (0,1)"
50	        TEXT cost_share_type "ENUM full_coverage|copay|coinsurance; discriminant"
51	        INTEGER copay_cents "cents; NOT NULL iff type=copay else NULL; CHECK >= 0"
52	        REAL coinsurance_rate "member share; NOT NULL iff type=coinsurance else NULL; CHECK 0..1"
53	        INTEGER applies_deductible "BOOL 0/1; CHECK IN (0,1)"
54	        TEXT limit_unit "ENUM none|dollars|visits; discriminant"
55	        INTEGER limit_amount_cents "cents; NOT NULL iff unit=dollars else NULL; CHECK > 0"
56	        INTEGER limit_count "visits; NOT NULL iff unit=visits else NULL; CHECK > 0"
57	        INTEGER requires_prior_auth "BOOL 0/1; CHECK IN (0,1)"
58	        TEXT created_at "ISO-8601 metadata"
59	        UK policy_service "UNIQUE(policy_id, service_code): 1 rule/service/policy"
60	    }
61	
62	    CLAIMS {
63	        TEXT id PK "UUID surrogate"
64	        TEXT member_id FK "to members.id; RESTRICT del / CASCADE upd"
65	        TEXT policy_id FK "to policies.id; RESTRICT del / CASCADE upd; recorded at intake"
66	        TEXT service_date "ISO-8601 date; GLOB-validated; CLAIM-level; drives policy-active"
67	        TEXT provider "PHI nullable; app-level encrypt; not adjudicated"
68	        TEXT diagnosis_code "PHI nullable; app-level encrypt; not adjudicated"
69	        TEXT status "ENUM SUBMITTED|UNDER_REVIEW|APPROVED|PARTIALLY_APPROVED|DENIED; derived"
70	        INTEGER claim_seq "claim-aggregate logical clock head; bumped per transition"
71	        TEXT created_at "ISO-8601 metadata"
72	        TEXT updated_at "ISO-8601 metadata"
73	    }
74	
75	    LINE_ITEMS {
76	        TEXT id PK "UUID surrogate"
77	        TEXT claim_id FK "to claims.id; CASCADE del / CASCADE upd"
78	        TEXT service_code "free text NOT NULL (no catalog CHECK); unlisted accepted at intake, NO_COVERAGE at adj (PRD:171)"
79	        INTEGER billed_cents "cents; CHECK > 0 (positive)"
80	        INTEGER units "CHECK > 0; default 1"
81	        INTEGER prior_auth_present "BOOL 0/1; default 1 (absence=present); CHECK IN (0,1)"
82	        TEXT status "ENUM PENDING|APPROVED|DENIED|NEEDS_REVIEW; PARTIALLY_APPROVED is claim-only"
83	        TEXT fingerprint "member_id+service_code+service_date+billed_cents; dup detection"
84	        TEXT created_at "ISO-8601 metadata"
85	        TEXT updated_at "ISO-8601 metadata"
86	    }
87	
88	    ADJUDICATIONS {
89	        TEXT id PK "UUID surrogate; APPEND-ONLY (triggers RAISE on INSERT-overwrite/UPDATE/DELETE)"
90	        TEXT line_item_id FK "to line_items.id; RESTRICT del / CASCADE upd"
91	        TEXT plan_year "accumulator window this decision wrote to; immutable audit anchor"
92	        INTEGER seq "logical clock; current = MAX(seq) per line_item_id"
93	        TEXT status "ENUM APPROVED|DENIED (line outcome of this decision)"
94	        INTEGER billed_cents "billed snapshot at decision time; CHECK > 0"
95	        INTEGER payable_cents "plan pays; CHECK >= 0"
96	        INTEGER member_responsibility_cents "member owes; CHECK >= 0"
97	        TEXT reasons_json "JSON ReasonCode[]; dominant first; CHECK json_type=array and non-empty"
98	        TEXT explanation "EOB sentence citing rule + numbers"
99	        INTEGER delta_deductible_inc_cents "accumulator delta written; CHECK >= 0"
100	        INTEGER delta_oop_inc_cents "accumulator delta written; CHECK >= 0"
101	        INTEGER delta_limit_inc "delta in rule unit (cents or count); CHECK >= 0"
102	        TEXT created_at "ISO-8601 metadata only; never read by logic"
103	        UK line_seq "UNIQUE(line_item_id, seq): deterministic ordering"
104	        UK id_line "UNIQUE(id, line_item_id): composite FK target for disputes"
105	    }
106	
107	    ACCUMULATORS {
108	        TEXT id PK "UUID surrogate; UPDATE-in-place (NOT append-only)"
109	        TEXT member_id FK "to members.id; RESTRICT del / CASCADE upd"
110	        TEXT plan_year "e.g. 2025; reset = new rows per plan_year"
111	        TEXT dimension "DEDUCTIBLE | OOP | 'LIMIT:'+service_code (free suffix)"
112	        TEXT unit "ENUM CENTS|COUNT; ties the populated column to the rule unit"
113	        INTEGER used_cents "for DEDUCTIBLE/OOP/dollar-limit; CHECK >= 0"
114	        INTEGER used_count "for visit-limit; CHECK >= 0"
115	        TEXT updated_at "ISO-8601 metadata"
116	        UK member_year_dim "UNIQUE(member_id, plan_year, dimension)"
117	    }
118	
119	    DISPUTES {
120	        TEXT id PK "UUID surrogate"
121	        TEXT line_item_id FK "to line_items.id; CASCADE del / CASCADE upd"
122	        TEXT original_adjudication_id FK "composite FK (id,line_item_id)->adjudications; NOT NULL; RESTRICT del"
123	        TEXT resolved_adjudication_id FK "composite FK (id,line_item_id)->adjudications; NULL while OPEN; RESTRICT del"
124	        TEXT reason "member rationale; surfaced verbatim; NOT NULL"
125	        INTEGER corrected_prior_auth_present "BOOL 0/1 nullable; only amendable field; CHECK IN (0,1)"
126	        TEXT corrected_service_code "nullable; only amendable field; free text (no catalog CHECK), mirrors line_items"
127	        INTEGER corrected_billed_cents "nullable; CHECK > 0; only amendable field"
128	        INTEGER corrected_units "nullable; CHECK > 0; only amendable field"
129	        TEXT outcome "ENUM UPHELD|OVERTURNED|PARTIALLY_OVERTURNED|MODIFIED; null while OPEN"
130	        TEXT state "ENUM OPEN|RESOLVED"
131	        TEXT opened_at "ISO-8601"
132	        TEXT resolved_at "ISO-8601 nullable (null while OPEN)"
133	        UK one_open "partial UNIQUE(line_item_id, original_adjudication_id) WHERE state=OPEN"
134	    }
135	
136	    STATUS_TRANSITIONS {
137	        TEXT id PK "UUID surrogate; APPEND-ONLY (triggers RAISE on INSERT-overwrite/UPDATE/DELETE)"
138	        TEXT entity_type "ENUM CLAIM|LINE_ITEM; discriminant"
139	        TEXT claim_id FK "to claims.id; the owning claim aggregate; always set; CASCADE del"
140	        TEXT line_item_id FK "to line_items.id; NOT NULL iff entity_type=LINE_ITEM; CASCADE del"
141	        TEXT from_status "nullable; null on create"
142	        TEXT to_status "new status; NOT NULL"
143	        TEXT actor "ENUM SYSTEM|MEMBER"
144	        TEXT reason "ENUM SUBMIT|ADJUDICATED|AGGREGATED|DISPUTE_REOPEN (coarse, not ReasonCode)"
145	        INTEGER seq "claim-aggregate logical clock; total order across the merged timeline"
146	        TEXT created_at "ISO-8601 metadata only; never read by logic"
147	        UK claim_seq "UNIQUE(claim_id, seq): one monotonic clock per claim aggregate"
148	    }
149	```
150	
151	> **Diagram note (dual FK, one edge).** `ADJUDICATIONS ||--o{ DISPUTES` is rendered as a single edge, but the DDL carries **two** FKs from `disputes` to `adjudications`: `original_adjudication_id` (NOT NULL, immutable, the challenged decision) and `resolved_adjudication_id` (NULLABLE, NULL while OPEN, the produced decision). Mermaid `erDiagram` collapses multiple edges between the same pair of entities to one, so the nullable-cardinality distinction lives in the DDL and the table reference below, not the picture. Both are **composite** FKs `(adjudication_id, line_item_id) -> adjudications(id, line_item_id)` so a dispute can only reference adjudications belonging to its own line item.
152

288	| — | | CHECK | `ck_disputes_state_shape` | OPEN→resolution NULL; RESOLVED→resolution fully populated |
289	
290	> Enforced by trigger (cross-row, not single-row CHECK): a `resolved_adjudication_id`, when set, must have a **higher `seq`** than `original_adjudication_id` (review finding #seq-order) — the resolved decision supersedes the challenged one.
